rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. G&P CONSTRUCTION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Answers To Firm Interrogatories And For Sanctions, Including Reasonable Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, May 30, 2014, Line1, DEFENDANT FINE BY DESIGN INC's motion To Compel Answers To Firm Interrogatories And For Sanctions, Including Reasonable Attorneys Fees Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff appears to have served full and complete responses to Form Interrogatories. The Motion is moot. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $ 500. CRC 3.1348(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 30, 2014, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant failed to provide courtesy copies of its moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B and the court's minute order issued on April 30, 2014. The parties are admonished to comply with the California Rules of Court. Specifically, for future filings, including courtesy copies, only one side of each page may be used. (CRC 2.102.) Further, all exhibits must be separated by exhibit tabs. (CRC 3.1110(f).) In its discretion, the Court may not consider future filings, including courtesy copies, that are not filed in compliance with the California Rules of Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, DBA BOSSA NOVA VS. AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Written Discovery And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, May 30, 2014, Line3, DEFENDANT AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC's motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Written Discovery And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part as follows: Plaintiff shall serve responses to Form Interrogatories as well as the documents requested by Request for Production of Documents, Set One¿numbers 1-7 and Request for Production of Documents, Set Two¿number 8 by June 10, 2014. All objections are waived. The Motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is denied without prejudice. It appears that a full and complete production of documents will answer the Special Interrogatories. If, following receipt of the documents, defendant determines that further responses to Special Interrogatories are due, the parties are ordered to meet and confer. If that fails to resolve the matter, then Defendant may bring a motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,495 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: JAMIE S. BLANZA VS. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fir Insurance Company Of Pittsburh Pa And Lotsolutions, Inc Person (S) Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 30, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA's Motion To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburh PA And Lotsolutions, Inc.'s Person(s) Most Knowledgeable. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. For the PMK depositions, the motion is denied. The parties have reached an agreement on the scheduling of the PMK depositions. National Union has produced its electronic claim file for plaintiff's claim in PDF format. As of May 5, 2014, National Union has also agreed to produce additional documents relating to plaintiff's claim, such as phone call records. For Request Nos. 2 - 5, 7 - 14, the motion is denied without prejudice; Plaintiff has not provided by declaration any specific facts establishing in the record the required good cause pursuant to CCP § 2025.450(b)(1) and (f). For Request nos. 6, 15 - 20, granted, in part, and denied, in part. Prior to production of responsive documents, the parties shall meet and confer and submit a stipulation and proposed protective order to the Court pertaining to the handling of confidential documents. The protective order shall limit the use and access to produced documents to the parties' counsel, support staff, witnesses, and expert witnesses in this action only. Nothing in the proposed protective order shall determine that any document marked "confidential" qualifies for sealing under CRC 2.550 or relieves any party from following the procedure in CRC 2.551. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BRENNAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 30, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and GEORGE GASCON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to cause of action 1 [retaliation (Labor Code sec. 1102.5-whistleblower)] is overruled as to CCSF and sustained without leave to amend as to George Gascon. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the violation of a state statute/s and the claim can only be pleaded against the employer. The demurrer to cause of action 2 [wrongful termination in violation of public policy (Labor Code sec. 1102.5)] is overruled because plaintiff sufficiently alleges a violation of Labor Code sec. 1102.5. The demurrer to cause of action 3 [wrongful termination in violation of public policy (Labor Code sec. 232.5] is overruled. The demurrer to causes of action 4 [negligent supervision], 5 [intentional infliction of emotional distress], and 6 [negligent infliction of emotional distress] are sustained without leave to amend as to CCSF. They are common law causes of action that are not actionable under the Government Claims Act. George Gascon's demurrers to causes of action 5 and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. See Miklosy v. the Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 902. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
